uUu3HOxVFmsWUvhl.name: Nualia’s Remains
uUu3HOxVFmsWUvhl.description: The wrapped bundle sitting atop the impromptu altar contains Nualia’s disarticulated skeleton, readily identifiable by its left arm: the bones are black and the hand appears as a large talon.
uUu3HOxVFmsWUvhl.gmDescription:
The goblins weren’t particularly gentle when they hauled her skeleton from the coffin, but the remains are whole and will serve well as a focus for a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gsYEuWv04XTDxe91]{Call Spirit} ritual.

Once the PCs recover Nualia’s remains, they can deliver them back to Abstalar Zantus in the cathedral above, but it’ll still be a few days before his friend Audrahni arrives to cast the call spirit ritual. In the meantime, he encourages the PCs to keep exploring, particularly if he learns about the catacombs, and asks the PCs if they can map the complex out for him while they explore and ensure that there’s nothing dangerous within.

VRy3AHiZupdXtn3G.name: Bronze Clockwork Songbird Wing
VRy3AHiZupdXtn3G.gmDescription: A bronze clockwork songbird wing once found at a ruin known as Raven’s Watch on the northern side of the Devil’s Platter. A doomed thief stole it from that ruin, only to be plucked up from the road on his way to town by the Sandpoint Devil, carried back here, and eaten. This wing is part of the clockwork songbird that, when completed, can activate the portal at area @UUID[JournalEntry.pf2ap20007apatte.JournalEntryPage.07singingdoor000]{H13}.

u41a5HM5nplYKQGo.name: Ilsoari’s Platinum Key
u41a5HM5nplYKQGo.description: A platinum key etched with the Sihedron rune but with a curious circle-shaped pattern in the middle.
u41a5HM5nplYKQGo.gmDescription: The symbol etched within the Sihedron on the key appears to be a circle surrounded by seven hooks—the symbol is likely a mystery to the PCs unless they’ve achieved at least 16 Research Points on the topic of @UUID[JournalEntry.pf2ap20012advent.JournalEntryPage.12researchingt00]{The Eighth Runelord}, in which case they’ll automatically recognize it as Liralarue’s rune of the sin of vainglory and a theoretical eighth school of Thassilonian magic associated with divination. This key can unlock the door to area @UUID[JournalEntry.pf2ap20007apatte.JournalEntryPage.07liralarueswo00]{H9}.

YQs3tTvZeC9rNSZB.name: Song of the Swamp
YQs3tTvZeC9rNSZB.description:
Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect The mobogo unleashes a booming croak. All boggards and mobogos within @Template[type:emanation|distance:50]{50 feet} gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls and saves against fear for 1 round. Other creatures in the area of effect must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:27] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for [[/br 1d4 #Duration Slowed]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for [[/br 1d4 #Duration Slowed]]{1d4 rounds}.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.hftFxE8JGJGzXAtU]{Effect: Song of the Swamp}

xPc5xxHX62bKWgkk.name: Tongue Grab
xPc5xxHX62bKWgkk.description:
A creature hit by the mobogo's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the mobogo. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the reach of the mobogo's tongue.

A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 27 that deals at least 10 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the mobogo but prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 round.

The mobogo can move without ending the Grab as long as the creature remains within the tongue's reach.

Tongue Grab.name: Tongue Grab
Tongue Grab.description:
A creature hit by the mobogo's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the mobogo. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the reach of the mobogo's tongue.

A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 27 that deals at least 10 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the mobogo but prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 round.

The mobogo can move without ending the Grab as long as the creature remains within the tongue's reach.

phtWMfKuIsMCi5cE.name: Tongue Reposition
phtWMfKuIsMCi5cE.description:
The mobogo attempts to move a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by its tongue. The mobogo rolls an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the creature's Fortitude DC.

On a success, the mobogo moves the creature into any space within the tongue's reach. If it wishes, the mobogo can transfer the grabbed creature to its jaws.

Tongue Reposition.name: Tongue Reposition

On a success, the mobogo moves the creature into any space within the tongue's reach. If it wishes, the mobogo can transfer the grabbed creature to its jaws.

SRv8Gjga6DHY4U8G.name: Corrupt Water
SRv8Gjga6DHY4U8G.description:
Frequency Once per day

Effect The dragon permanently befouls 10 cubic feet of liquid within 90 feet. The liquid becomes undrinkable and unable to support water-breathing life.

This destroys liquid magic or alchemical items if they're of a lower level than the dragon (a creature can attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:31] save to protect liquids in its possession). This doesn't affect liquids in a creature's body.

6h9jexgK3OdaDWsq.name: Peace Vulnerability
6h9jexgK3OdaDWsq.description: A vrock's wrath is the heart of their essence, and forcing peace upon them wrenches at their soul. If they fail a save against @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.b515AZlB0sridKSq]{Calm} or a similar effect forcing them to be peaceful, a vrock takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage.

pSi2yAlBjhVTBwq8.name: Dance of Ruin
pSi2yAlBjhVTBwq8.description:
The vrock dances in flight and chants to create a ruinous explosion of electricity. If more vrocks within 30 feet want to join the dance, the vrock can delay the dance to wait for more vrocks; in that case, the effect occurs after the last vrock uses Dance of Ruin or one of the vrocks chooses to complete that round of the dance. Each non-demon creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] from any of the dancing vrocks takes @Damage[2d12[electricity]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true] save). For each additional vrock that joins the dance, the damage increases by 1d12 and the save DC increases by 1 (to a maximum of four vrocks dealing 5d12 electricity damage with a DC 31 save). The vrocks can continue dancing by using Dance of Ruin each round, for up to 3 rounds in total. The emanation's size increases by 20 feet each round, and the damage increases by 1d12 per vrock each round.

  • 1 Vrock Dancing
    Round Effect
    1 @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] @Damage[2d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true]
    2 @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] @Damage[3d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true]
    3 @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] @Damage[4d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true]
  • 2 Vrocks Dancing
    Round Effect
    1 @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] @Damage[3d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:29|basic:true]
    2 @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] @Damage[5d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:29|basic:true]
    3 @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] @Damage[7d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:29|basic:true]
  • 3 Vrocks Dancing
    Round Effect
    1 @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] @Damage[4d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:30|basic:true]
    2 @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] @Damage[7d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:30|basic:true]
    3 @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] @Damage[10d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:30|basic:true]
  • 4 Vrocks Dancing
    Round Effect
    1 @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] @Damage[5d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:31|basic:true]
    2 @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] @Damage[9d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:31|basic:true]
    3 @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] @Damage[13d12[electricity]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:31|basic:true]

rCo3WVukDgsbPhLD.name: Spore Cloud
rCo3WVukDgsbPhLD.description:
The vrock emits a cloud of spores from their body, dealing @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage to all adjacent creatures. Each creature damaged this way must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:28|traits:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[2d8[persistent,piercing]] damage as the spores penetrate its skin and grow into thick, green vines. The vines cease growing after 10 rounds, and they wither away in [[/br 1d4 #Spore Cloud Duration]]{1d4 days} if not shaved off before then. The vines can be destroyed if the creature is affected by a good spell or if @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.z9T4c1hXwOotsMCp]{Holy Water} is applied to the vines (with an Interact action).

Once the vrock uses Spore Cloud, the ability can't be used for [[/br 1d6 #Recharge Spore Cloud]]{1d6 rounds}.

ymEtt6PtcG7bIALW.name: Foul Concoction
ymEtt6PtcG7bIALW.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Aliver quickly mixes alchemical catalysts and materials together, creating a burst of foul-smelling smoke that fills a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10]. The cloud functions as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.GKpEcy9WId6NJvtx]{Stinking Cloud} (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:25]) except that Aliver is immune to the putrid stench created by the concoction.

Frequency once per day

NeK5f2GEdedgA4SI.name: Precise Poisoning
NeK5f2GEdedgA4SI.description: Aliver applies a dose of poison (typically a dose of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.aBOPYlfHAcXUmhF7]{Giant Wasp Venom}) to a bolt on his crossbow, then takes careful aim with the poisoned bolt and attempts a ranged Strike with his hand crossbow. If he hits, he inflicts an additional 1d6 piercing damage with the bolt as well as the poison effect.

lKCBlYHM1b1jr4kU.name: Devil's Breath
lKCBlYHM1b1jr4kU.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The ascended disciple cuts their tongue with a slashing weapon held in a hand (taking @Damage[1[slashing]] damage), then exhales a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:22] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[2d8[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the agonizing pain of the burns.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d8[fire]] damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} by the smoke for 1 minute.

abGmJgduMKED2VeX.name: Chupar
abGmJgduMKED2VeX.description:
Requirements The chupacabra has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}

Effect The chupacabra sucks blood from the grabbed creature. The chupacabra gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} condition for 1 minute and can use the extra action only for Strike and Stride actions. A chupacabra can't use Chupar again while it is quickened in this way.

A creature that has its blood drained by a chupacabra is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} until it receives healing (of any kind or amount).

cqrFa6Vv4ysyzmgT.name: Wing Flurry
cqrFa6Vv4ysyzmgT.description: The false devil's spiny wings don't afford it a typical melee option, but it can use them to scrape and slash at foes it flies by. The winged chupacabra attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.21WIfSu7Xd7uKqV8]{Tumble Through} an enemy's space. As it does so, it flaps its wings furiously, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its Acrobatics check. If it succeeds, it inflicts @Damage[4d6[slashing]] damage to the creature it Tumbled Through (or @Damage[8d6[slashing]] damage on a critical success).

vVucSfThIT1iEEjO.description:
Requirements A creature adjacent to the marsh giant is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} in water at least 1 foot deep

Effect The marsh giant uses its gaff to shake the creature back and forth and hold it underwater. The giant attempts an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the target's Fortitude DC.

Critical Success The target takes @Damage[(6d6+14)[piercing]] damage and loses 5 rounds' worth of air if they were holding their breath.

Success The target takes @Damage[(3d6+14)[piercing]] damage and loses 3 rounds' worth of air if they were holding their breath.

Failure The target is unaffected.

Critical Failure As failure, but the giant drops its gaff as well.

91iOrkVbzeP7cK2M.name: Adhesive
91iOrkVbzeP7cK2M.description:
The mimic is coated in an adhesive slime. Any creature that hits the mimic, is hit by the mimic, or otherwise touches the mimic must succeed at a @Check[type:reflex|dc:23] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} (Escape DC 23). A weapon that hits the mimic is stuck to the mimic and can be removed with a successful @Check[type:athletics|dc:23] check (made as a single action).

A mimic can have any number of objects or creatures stuck to it at a time. The mimic can release a stuck creature or object by using a single action, and the adhesive dissolves 1 minute after the mimic dies, releasing all stuck objects and creatures.

Ns2pok7e6UtuKUd6.name: Object Lesson
Ns2pok7e6UtuKUd6.description:
Trigger A creature touches or physically interacts with the mimic while the mimic is transformed using Mimic Object.

Effect The triggering creature is automatically stuck by the mimic's adhesive (it receives no save). The mimic then makes a pseudopod Strike against any creature adjacent to the mimic.

Object Lesson can't be used again until the mimic escapes and takes on a new disguise.

2JiFczs0m1iIKqPZ.name: Flurry of Strands

Each attack must be against a different target. These attacks count toward the roper's multiple attack penalty, but its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.

H85aThyyHpy8z06u.name: Pull the Strands
H85aThyyHpy8z06u.description: The roper pulls every creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by its strands toward itself. The roper rolls a single @Check[type:athletics] check and compares the result to each @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} creature's Fortitude DC. The roper pulls each creature it succeeds against up to 25 feet closer and each creature it critically succeeds against up to 50 feet closer.

FyUL1Dfsy8YJIJH4.name: Sticky Strand
FyUL1Dfsy8YJIJH4.description: Any creature hit by a roper's strand is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. Each additional hit from a strand increases the enfeebled condition value by 1 (to a maximum of enfeebled 4). This enfeebled value decreases by 1 every 8 hours. The roper can move while it has a creature grabbed with a strand, but it automatically releases the creature if it moves beyond the strand's reach. The roper can release an grabbed creature as a free action. A creature can sever a strand with a target attack that hits AC 27 and deals at least 18 slashing damage. This doesn't deal any damage to the roper itself, though it can no longer attack with a severed strand.

dpMfwwbltlZ55vSZ.name: Final Blasphemy
dpMfwwbltlZ55vSZ.description:
When the herexen is destroyed, it explodes in a wave of void energy with the effects of a 3-action, 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} spell (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:26|basic:true]).

The herexen is destroyed, so it doesn't gain any Hit Points from this use of harm, and it doesn't need to have any harm spells remaining to use this ability.

l5DvOnScyCXWCLxu.name: Swarming Curse
l5DvOnScyCXWCLxu.description: Hundreds of biting flies crawl over Narlo's bones, filling out his armor almost like his flesh once did. When these flies burn, they harm Narlo as well, imparting weakness to fire 5. Whenever Narlo casts a spell, these flies burst from his body to fill a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] around him in a buzzing, biting cloud until the start of his next turn. During this time, he loses his weakness to fire, and any creature that ends their turn within the emanation takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:23|basic:true] save) from the biting flies.

E4osyN7mgvABXIRE.description:
Frequency three times a day

Trigger A creature within 30 feet of the Red Bishop speaks Pazuzu's name aloud three times with a single breath

Effect The Red Bishop casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Charm} on the creature. A creature with a fly speed takes a –4 circumstance penalty to the saving throw against this charm.

2IybDR9dBLK8bfWV.name: Agent of Fate
2IybDR9dBLK8bfWV.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The mothman casts any spell of 5th level or lower that normally takes 2 or fewer actions to cast, whether they have those spells in their innate spells or not.

They can use this ability only if doing so steers the flow of fate in its proper course toward a disaster or otherwise significantly memorable event.

What the proper flow of fate entails is determined by the GM, but some examples include casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ucf8eynbZMfUucjE]{Illusory Scene} to coax someone to a portentous location, casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.R9xqCBblkS5KE4y7]{Sending} to deliver an important message to someone the exact moment they need to hear it, or casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.0fYE64odlKqISzft]{Rusting Grasp} to weaken a structure and cause some necessary calamity.

ZZeKvjZOeEkU4d1b.description: The Red Bishop's telepathic words carry an unsettling weight and promise of punishment when spoken to any evil creature capable of flight. Against such creatures, the Red Bishop gains a +3 circumstance bonus to attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.DCb62iCBrJXy0Ik6]{Request}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.tHCqgwjtQtzNqVvd]{Coerce}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize}, and he increases the DC of any mental effect he uses against such creatures by 3 as well. Even without these bonuses, evil flying creatures tend to treat the Red Bishop with a mix of respect, if not outright fear.

Wm8dymSLkG00xtyc.name: Extract Fear

The Red Bishop reaches out with one hand toward a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} creature he can see that's within 30 feet and makes a clutching motion to pull out the creature's fear and feed upon it. The Red Bishop restores [[/r (7d8+8)[healing]]] Hit Points to himself, and the target creature loses the frightened condition but must also attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:34] save as other emotions are drawn out of their mind as well to fade uselessly away, resulting in mental trauma and a strange numbness in the mind itself.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage.

Failure The target takes @Damage[6d6[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[12d6[mental]] damage and is stupefied 1 until the condition is removed via an effect like restoration.

The mothman fixes their glare at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against the mothman's portentous gaze.

If the creature is already frightened when it attempts this save, on a failure it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours in addition to the frightened effect.

After attempting its save, the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

0Qt9ULbGzJV8ZG3W.name: Shadow Spawn
0Qt9ULbGzJV8ZG3W.description: When a creature's shadow is pulled free by Steal Shadow, it becomes a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.XrmHgbKgcHDi4OnK]{Shadow Spawn} under the command of the shadow that created it. This shadow spawn doesn't have Steal Shadow and is perpetually and incurably @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}. If the creature the shadow spawn was pulled from dies, the shadow spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous shadow. If the creature recovers from its @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeeblement}, its shadow returns to it and the shadow spawn is extinguished.

Kjifw1jgDvfmrEGU.name: Steal Shadow
Kjifw1jgDvfmrEGU.description:
Requirements The shadow hit a living creature with a shadow hand Strike on its previous action.

Effect The shadow pulls at the target's shadow, making the creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}. This is cumulative with other enfeebled conditions from shadows, to a maximum of enfeebled 4. If this increases a creature's enfeebled value to 3 or more, the target's shadow is separated from its body. Enfeebled from Steal Shadow decreases by 1 every hour.

3y4grxqduw8smrrg.name: Rejuvenation
3y4grxqduw8smrrg.description: Destroying the Spectral Devil releases its pure, evil essence into the world, where it can find a new body and re-form. When the Spectral Devil is destroyed, its essence finds the remains of a dead horse and rebuilds its physical body over the course of [[/br 1d10 #days]]{1d10 days}. Only completing a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dbd5W6G8U2vzWolN]{Consecrate} ritual heightened to at least 6th level, which consecrates the site of the Spectral Devil's destruction to a good deity, can prevent the Spectral Devil from returning and haunting the world once more.

myfzzx054if3u6rg.name: Accursed Shriek
myfzzx054if3u6rg.description:
The Spectral Devil unleashes a terrifying whinny in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30]{30-foot cone} that deals @Damage[5d10[sonic]] damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:29] save.

The Spectral Devil can't use Accursed Shriek for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Accursed Shriek]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} by the eerie noise.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, is frightened 2, and becomes cursed. The victim's mind echoes with the sound of the whinny, impairing concentration. The cursed creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} until the curse is removed.

f33ibm0weYFRsbLf.name: Spray Webbing
f33ibm0weYFRsbLf.description:
Trigger A creature steps on the pressure plate adjacent to the sticky greeter

Effect The sticky greeter lurches as its abdomen snaps forward, opens, and sprays the triggering creature with thick sheets of alchemical webbing. At the same time, the sticky greeter emits a high-pitched whistling sound. The creature must make a @Check[type:reflex|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and held in place, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} on a critical failure until it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escapes} (DC 22) or the webbing binding it is destroyed.

up0HVVMUk86V0DdT.description:
The striding fire Strides up to double its Speed in a straight line. Its movement during this Stride doesn't trigger reactions. Any creature the striding fire was adjacent to at any point during this Stride must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:24|basic:true] save. If it critically fails, it is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} by a wave of heated air.

The striding fire can't use Burning Rush for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Burning Rush]]{1d4 rounds}.

hIxE6LMA92SPk1eC.name: Death Flash
hIxE6LMA92SPk1eC.description:
When the caligni creeper dies, their body combusts in a flash of bright light.

All creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:17] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for [[/br 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds}.

Creatures with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.I0HYG0ctCLP5JRsW]{Light Blindness} who successfully save are still blinded for 1 round.

The creeper's gear and treasure are left in a pile where they died. As this isn't a magical effect, the light has no effect within magical darkness.

The cursed creature takes a -2 status penalty to all checks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.

fHam88iT0r3U5tcl.description: A group of six jinkins can work together for an hour to imbue an item with a curse at a range of 60 feet. While this process is lengthy, it's also unobtrusive and can be performed while @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Hiding}. Jinkins prefer to use this ability on magic items. The curse makes the item unreliable (@Check[type:flat|dc:5] check or waste any action to Interact with or Activate the item), adds a bizarre requirement to use the item, or imparts some other curse of a similar caliber.

Trigger Gorlak's previous action was a successful Strike on a creature with his ogre hook

Effect Gorlak twists his hook to embed it in the creature by attempting an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the target's Fortitude DC. On a success, that creature takes @Damage[(1d10+9)[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:25|basic:true] save) and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until Gorlak attempts another Strike with his Ogre Hook or attempts to Fling Foe the creature.

OCQJPv0SiHaB7c4b.name: Fling Foe
OCQJPv0SiHaB7c4b.description:
Requirements Gorlak's last action was to Embed Hook in a Medium or smaller creature

Effect Gorlak attempts to fling the creature embedded on his hook through the air by attempting an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the embedded creature's Fortitude DC.

Critical Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:25|basic:true] save) as the hook rips free and is hurled 15 feet away, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} in a space of Gorlak's choosing.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+9)[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:25|basic:true] save) as the hook rips free and is hurled 5 feet away, becoming prone.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[1d10[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:25|basic:true] save) as the hook rips free and is left standing in a space adjacent to Gorlak.

Critical Failure The creature takes no damage as the hook rips free. It is left standing in a space adjacent to Gorlak, and Gorlak becomes so frustrated by his failure that he must use his first action on his next turn to curse and scream and shake his fists in the air.

A wood golem targeted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.CXICME10TkEJxz0P]{Shape Wood} takes [[/r {2d8}]]{2d8 damage} per spell rank, with a basic Fortitude save against the caster's spell DC.

On a critical failure, the golem is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for [[/br 1d4 #Shape Wood Immobilization]]{1d4 rounds}.

D1R03gCQbWQLLgtL.name: Splinter Volley
D1R03gCQbWQLLgtL.description:
The wood golem makes up to four splinter Strikes, each against a different target.

These attacks count toward the wood golem's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the wood golem makes all of its attacks.

lciayGn2ma9dSbcM.name: Curse of Darkness
lciayGn2ma9dSbcM.description:
The owb inflicts a curse on one creature taking persistent cold damage from their burning cold Strike, stealing the victim's vibrancy. The creature must attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:23] save.

On a failure, the creature gains @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.I0HYG0ctCLP5JRsW]{Light Blindness} and its coloration turns to washed out shades of gray, along with all equipment it carries, wields, or wears. These effects have an unlimited duration. Regardless of the result of its save, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

If the owb uses this ability on a caligni, the curse can't be removed short of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish} or similar powerful magic.

voG8n2irt09cUOF7.name: Sacrifice Pawn
voG8n2irt09cUOF7.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Vizmivool attempts a dagger Strike against an ally and shifts the result of this Strike up by one degree of success. If the Strike kills the ally, the sacrificed ally's life force flows like red fire out of the wound and into Vizmivool's body, healing her for @Damage[10d6[healing]] Hit Points and making her @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} for 1 minute. She can use the additional action to Strike or Stride.

dyNhPooeY6ACzEdg.name: Devil's Breath
dyNhPooeY6ACzEdg.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The Devil's Disciple cuts their tongue with a slashing weapon held in a hand (taking @Damage[1[slashing]] damage), then exhales a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d8[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the agonizing pain of the burns.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[fire]] damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} by the smoke for 1 minute.

VRDZl6y3aAge5VyP.name: Raise Serpent
VRDZl6y3aAge5VyP.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The bone prophet animates corpses of snakes, serpentfolk, or similar serpentine creatures within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Any flesh on the bodies sloughs off, and they rise as skeletons. The bone prophet can raise one Large creature as a skeletal giant or up to three Medium creatures as skeletal champions; the equipment and attacks might be different depending on the corpses' possessions.

These skeletons have the minion trait and are under the bone prophet's control; the bone prophet can give all these minions the same command with a single action that has the concentrate trait. Any skeletal minions that still remain after 10 minutes crumble to dust.

dkfqFFDoPeLbQKNp.name: Construct Armor (Hardness 7)
dkfqFFDoPeLbQKNp.description:
Like normal objects, an animated statue has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness.

Once an animated statue is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and its Armor Class is reduced to 15.

JRw4yyJi23UZoHax.name: Distracting Whistle
JRw4yyJi23UZoHax.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Tunch whistles in a way that causes the sound to manifest elsewhere, potentially distracting a foe. One target of Tunch's choice that he can see within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of Tunch's next turn by the distracting whistling sound that seems to come from behind them. The target is then temporarily immune to Distracting Whistle for 1 hour.

PhDx2NxQaVTrJgx5.name: Quick Bomber
PhDx2NxQaVTrJgx5.description: Tunch Interacts to draw a bomb, then Strikes with it.

RGaUdIjwMpgnNYGg.name: Pain Starvation
RGaUdIjwMpgnNYGg.description: A specter that goes for more than a month without dealing void damage to a living humanoid becomes desperate and almost feral. It changes alignment from lawful evil to chaotic evil, loses control of any corrupted thralls it might have, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened}. It can use its additional action only to make vile touch Strikes against humanoid targets. At the end of any turn in which it deals any amount of void damage to a living humanoid, it reverts to lawful evil and is no longer quickened, but any thralls it lost control of remain free.

qh8LFXVLWjILfZZx.name: Sunlight Powerlessness
qh8LFXVLWjILfZZx.description: A specter caught in sunlight is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for as long as it remains in the sunlight.

OSiq1CXChzTT7udW.name: Spectral Corruption
OSiq1CXChzTT7udW.description:
The specter makes a Vile Touch Strike. If it damages a living creature, the specter gains [[/r 5 #Temporary Hit Points]]{5 temporary Hit Points} and the target creature must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:24] save to avoid becoming corrupted.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to spectral corruption for 1 minute.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 hour.

Failure The creature succumbs to the corruption and becomes a spectral thrall temporarily. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled} by the specter, obeying the specter's telepathic or spoken orders, though a spectral thrall does not obey obviously self-destructive orders. This lasts until the end of the thrall's next turn, at which point it is no longer controlled but becomes stupefied 2 for 1 hour.

Critical Failure As failure, but the duration is unlimited. The thrall can attempt a new Will save at the end of each of its turns; on a success, it is no longer controlled by the specter but becomes stupefied 2 for 1 hour.

c3gUtrnAh1QoPfD4.name: Fearful Cry
c3gUtrnAh1QoPfD4.description:
Trigger A creature walks into view of the skulls and is spotted by them

Effect The candles atop all six skulls flare to light and the skulls shriek out fearsome, mind-numbing howls. All creatures in area F6, the stairs, or the eastern trough must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:25] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} as long as they remain frightened.

Critical Failure As failure, but once the creature is no longer frightened, the previous minute of memories are erased—likely removing knowledge of the secret door at the top of the stairs in area E7a. All that remains are strange lingering fears about returning to the area.

YHkXKmipljWLHYIU.name: Grim Curse of Azathoth
YHkXKmipljWLHYIU.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Iazmilor invokes Azathoth's name in Aklo as he stares at a creature he can see within 30 feet. His eyes glow orange, and the target endures a brief vision of the seething chaos that churns at the center of the Material Plane and, for that instant, beholds their own insignificance in the cosmos. The target must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour.

Failure The creature becomes stupefied 1, and the first time each night they gaze upon the night sky, they must make a @Check[type:will|dc:26] save or they increase the value of their stupefied condition by 1 (to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}, or they become stupefied 1 if they do not have this condition). Even if the stupefied condition is removed, the target must continue to save upon gazing upon the night sky once per night until the curse is removed.

Aow1y5tVJE1v7Dng.name: Protect the Stone
Aow1y5tVJE1v7Dng.description:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door or damages the door with a Strike or spell

Effect The door whispers into the triggering creature's ear, speaking in one of the following three languages: Shoanti, Thassilonian, or Varisian. If the target creature speaks any of these languages, it hears the whispers in the tongue it is most comfortable speaking. If the target does not speak these languages, the trap's whispers have no effect. If the target understands the whispers, the trap casts suggestion on the target (@Check[type:will|dc:25] save), suggesting they "stand guard before the door and fight any who attempt to pass by it to enter the chamber beyond until Mistress Liralarue's arrival." Once, the whispers also alerted Liralarue, but as she is long gone now, the PCs don't need to fear the arrival of a powerful runelord's apprentice.

uxi7mZBlBZgzITyC.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Asmerelli bites down on her tongue (taking @Damage[1[slashing]] damage), then exhales a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d8[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d8[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the agonizing pain of the burns.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d8[fire]] damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and becomes cursed. The victim's burns never fully vanish, and the cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all attacks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.

mP5Cg1iQrikWq4cw.name: Sickly
mP5Cg1iQrikWq4cw.description:
Cruncher is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} due to his mistreatment from the goblins. In addition, his eyes are rheumy and caked with gunk, resulting in a –4 circumstance penalty to sight-based Perception checks and causing him to be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} beyond a range of 5 feet. If he escapes into the wild, he heals back up after a few nights' rest and a big meal. Alternatively, a PC can speed his recovery with a @Check[type:medicine|dc:20] check, with the results detailed below; this takes 10 minutes to perform, and as such Cruncher needs to either be helpless or willing to receive the attention.

Critical Success Cruncher's vision clears and he loses the enfeebled condition.

Success Cruncher's vision clears, and he'll lose the enfeebled condition after 8 hours of rest.

Failure The attempt provides no aid.

Critical Failure As failure, but Cruncher takes @Damage[2d6] points of damage as well, potentially causing him to lose his trust in the PCs and driving him to attack.

x3sRV0Fub8ixWfBV.name: Toss
x3sRV0Fub8ixWfBV.description:
The hodag Strides, then makes a Strike against a target in reach. If it moves at least 20 feet and succeeds at its Strike, the hodag deals damage normally and then attempts an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the creature's Fortitude DC to toss the enemy into the air.

On a success, the tossed creature is thrown 10 feet in a straight line in the direction of the hodag's choice and then lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the creature is knocked into a solid object, it takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage as well before landing prone. The hodag can instead toss a creature straight up in the air. The creature lands in the same square where it started, takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage, and lands prone.

Bh4tXIFDTCdh3Vzs.name: Breath Weapon
Bh4tXIFDTCdh3Vzs.description:
The brimorak spits their boiling blood in a @Template[type:line|distance:20] that deals @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:21|basic:true] save).

The brimorak can't use their Breath Weapon again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Breath Weapon]]{1d4 rounds}.

The ground within this area becomes slippery, with the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wu0xFpewMKRK3HG8]{Grease} spell targeting an area until it's cleaned up or the brimorak is able to use their Breath Weapon again, whichever comes first.

A brimorak's hooves and any weapon they wield burst into flame, dealing an extra 1d6 fire damage with each hit.

The brimorak emits a cloud of thick black smoke in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] that remains in place for [[/br 1d4 #Duration Fume]]{1d4 rounds}.

All creatures within the smoke become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the smoke it must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

Sg3CFomZepZdIWcH.name: Death Gaze

If the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained}, the bodak gains a number of temporary Hit Points equal to 5 times the value of the drained condition the target gained.

Multiple exposures to this ability can increase a creature's drained condition to a maximum of 4. If the bodak is destroyed, any @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed} condition a creature has gained from Death Gaze are removed.

Effect Shadowy tendrils wisp up from the darkness below to brush against the triggering creature's body. The creature must attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:30] save or their strength is sapped from their body. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature's speeds are reduced by 10 feet for 1 round.

Failure As success, but the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Effect The totenmaske drains flesh from the creature's body. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} unless it succeeds at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] save (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).

JHlhwEGUybavtbTq.name: Living Form
JHlhwEGUybavtbTq.description:
The totenmaske takes the appearance of a Medium or smaller humanoid creature. This is either its form from before it became undead, or the form of the last creature it successfully hit with Drink Flesh.

This doesn't change the totenmaske's Speed or the attack and damage bonuses for its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

DteZBAInF0ur5LIY.name: Shape Flesh
DteZBAInF0ur5LIY.description:
After spending 1 minute in contact with a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or willing creature, a totenmaske can reshape the target's face, causing flesh to cover vital features.

The target can attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] save to resist; a critical success grants temporary immunity to Shape Flesh for 24 hours.

Each time the totenmaske Shapes Flesh, it chooses one feature: ears (target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened}), eyes (target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded}), mouth (target can't speak or eat), or nose (target can't smell). A creature with both its nose and mouth sealed can't breathe and begins to suffocate.

Changes are permanent until reversed by removing this curse, or the sealed flesh can be surgically opened with a @Check[type:medicine|dc:25] check that takes [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} and deals @Damage[1d6[slashing]] damage per round.

IJnSeb9b6BHfammW.name: Bat-scorpion-rat-spider Taxidermy
IJnSeb9b6BHfammW.description: Tunch’s bat-scorpion-rat-spider taxidermy is, despite (or perhaps because of) its unsettling nature, rather well put together.
IJnSeb9b6BHfammW.gmDescription: Worth 45 gp to someone like Vorvashali Voon of the Feathered Serpent in Sandpoint (area @UUID[JournalEntry.pf2ap20011sandpo.JournalEntryPage.11thefeathered00]{44}), who swiftly puts it on sale, claiming the creature was brought to Golarion from the red planet Akiton before it was stuffed.

9uDPkhvDHQvs18xG.name: Viviparous Birth
9uDPkhvDHQvs18xG.description:
When killed, an empress bore worm violently expels the young it carries. These young erupt as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-3.Actor.aQusm2Uh1tw00IVb]{Bore Worm Swarm} in the empress bore worm's space.

In addition, every creature within @Template[type:emanation|distance:20]{20 feet} takes @Damage[5d10[acid]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:25|basic:true] save) from the splatter of caustic viscera.

8jAtUvdgKdyaNzHN.description:

A creature that enters or begins its turn in such a square takes @Damage[3d6[acid]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:22|basic:true] save). The acid becomes inert after 1 minute.

co4QqqzbDABHUIAu.name: Activate Serpent Mode
co4QqqzbDABHUIAu.description:
Trigger The clockwork serpent begins its turn

Effect The clockwork serpent transforms into a different mode. Roll 1d3 and consult the options below. If it rolls a mode it can't currently activate, the clockwork serpent becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the start of its next turn. At the end of its turn, it automatically deactivates its serpent mode.

1 Cobra Mode The clockwork serpent can use Intimidating Hood.

2 Constrictor Mode The clockwork serpent can use its coils Strike and can Constrict.

3 Rattlesnake Mode The clockwork serpent can use Shattering Rattle.

On a failure, it takes the damage from the critical hit as normal, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}, and its serpent mode deactivates.

BaTacjtQvnaLoVc5.name: Wind-Up
BaTacjtQvnaLoVc5.description:
24 hours, @Check[type:thievery|traits:action:disable-a-device|dc:22], standby

For a clockwork to act, it must be wound with a unique key by another creature. This takes 1 minute. Once wound, it remains operational for the listed amount of time, usually 24 hours, after which time it becomes unaware of its surroundings and can't act until it's wound again. Some clockworks' abilities require them to spend some of their remaining operational time. They can't spend more than they have and shut down immediately once they have 0 time remaining. If it's unclear when a clockwork was last wound, most clockwork keepers wind all their clockworks at a set time, typically 8 a.m.

A clockwork that lists standby in its wind-up entry can enter standby mode as a 3-action activity. Its operational time doesn't decrease in standby, but it can sense its surroundings (with a -2 penalty to Perception). It can't act, with one exception: when it perceives a creature, it can exit standby as a reaction (rolling initiative if appropriate).

A creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable a Device} to wind a clockwork down (with a DC listed in the wind-up entry). For each success, the clockwork loses 1 hour of operational time. This can be done even if the clockwork is in standby mode.

jZf3j2xK5YKD3uC0.name: Activate Defenses
jZf3j2xK5YKD3uC0.description:
One of the soldier's external plates extends on a mechanical actuator to defend the soldier or an adjacent creature of the soldier's choice.

The creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of the soldier's next turn, or until it is no longer adjacent to the soldier, whichever comes first. The soldier can have no more than one plate extended at a time.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.ya8r3IdzQ7QGNH8N]{Effect: Activate Defenses}

ikgvETyW4jWL1kPC.name: Ghostly Murder
ikgvETyW4jWL1kPC.description:
Jervis Stoot raises a hand high, and a murder of ghostly crows manifests in a vortex above his hand. He then lowers his hand to point in a direction, and the murder of ghostly crows swarms out in a @Template[type:cone|distance:20]. All creatures caught in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:30] save. Jervis can't use Ghostly Murder again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Ghostly Murder]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[6d6[slashing]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[10d6[slashing],2d6[bleed]]{10d6 slashing damage and 2d6 bleed} damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[15d6[slashing],5d6[bleed]]{15d6 slashing damage, 5d6 bleed} damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded}. The blindness persists until it's healed via an effect like restore senses.

DD1VjmyNLGVF3IHp.name: Toxic Effluvium
DD1VjmyNLGVF3IHp.description: Kanker has exposed himself to so many toxins and poisons over the years that it's all seeped into his undead flesh. When he activates Toxic Effluvium, these foul-smelling fluids seep from his flesh, causing his jaws and claws to secrete drooling rivulets of foul green venom that coat his teeth and talons, as well as any weapon he wields. Until the start of his next turn, Kanker's melee Strikes deal an additional 2d6 poison damage.

fH4zN6EufktPqbW4.name: Clockwork Wand
fH4zN6EufktPqbW4.description:
The clockwork mage uses a mechanical wand as a focus to channel magical energy. This wand is built into the clockwork mage's chest, with only the crystal at the end exposed. The mage can Interact to the remove the wand, or someone else can remove it with a @Check[type:thievery|dc:31|traits:action:disable-a-device] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable a Device}. The clockwork mage becomes unable to cast any spells except cantrips while the wand is removed.

When removed, the clockwork wand is a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.vJZ49cgi8szuQXAD]{Magic Wand} containing the last 2nd-rank innate spell the clockwork mage cast (the GM determines the spell randomly if it has not cast any eligible spells). The spells are placed within the wand while the mage is created, and the creator can substitute other arcane spells of the appropriate rank.

Frequency once per 10 minutes.

Effect The clockwork mage regains a spell it has already cast that day. It must spend 1 hour of its operational time, or 2 hours if the spell is 3rd rank or higher.

Frequency once per 10 minutes.

zZdlKoZukVTRu2lC.name: Cavern Dependent
zZdlKoZukVTRu2lC.description:
A lampad is mystically bonded to a single cavern or other self-contained underground area and must remain within 300 feet of it.

If they move beyond that range, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} and are unable to recover. They must attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:19] save every hour or increase the sickened value by 1 (to a maximum of sickened 4).

After 24 hours, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}, with this value increasing by 1 every additional 24 hours. A lampad can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond to a new cavern.

Cavern Dependent.name: Cavern Dependent

Mq1buiTxEF2rhIx8.name: Weep
Mq1buiTxEF2rhIx8.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The lampad begins a heart-wrenching fit of weeping, inspiring sympathetic sobbing in nearby creatures. Each non-lampad creature within @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} who hears the lampad's weeping must succeed at a @Check[type:will|dc:20] save or be unable to use reactions for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} on its next turn as it sobs uncontrollably.

KJpdd8KkjcqEPnoa.name: Pummeling Flurry

CXVQlp8TsdJY16il.name: Snatch Weapon
CXVQlp8TsdJY16il.description: Chertus attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} a creature. q9qnT4OPhxU6cbs9.name: Devil's Cursed Breath
q9qnT4OPhxU6cbs9.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Jordus bites down on his tongue (taking @Damage[1[slashing]] damage), then exhales a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d8[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[7d8[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the agonizing pain of the burns.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[10d8[fire]] damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and becomes cursed. The victim's burns never fully vanish, and the cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all attacks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.khL2A1slsnufpJp4]{Effect: Devil's Cursed Breath}

WKWzTrWyiPJNihmZ.name: Thoughtsense

dYvQOwzgoNuS9cG8.name: Body Thief
dYvQOwzgoNuS9cG8.description:
The intellect devourer reduces in size and burrows into the brain of a creature dead for no longer than 1 day. At the start of the intellect devourer's next turn, the body revives at its maximum Hit Points, controlled by the devourer. The intellect devourer is conscious and can sense everything the possessed body could.

Any effect that ends the possession kills the host body with the same effects as Exit Body. The intellect devourer can't use any of the host creature's spells with Body Thief but can use its own spells.

The host body slowly decays while inhabited by the intellect devourer, becoming uninhabitable after 7 days unless preserved with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xRgU9rrhmGAgG4Rc]{Peaceful Rest} or a similar effect.

Body Thief.name: Body Thief
Body Thief.description:

At the start of the intellect devourer's next turn, the body revives at its maximum Hit Points, controlled by the devourer. The intellect devourer is conscious and can sense everything the possessed body could.

Any effect that ends the possession kills the host body with the same effects as Exit Body. The intellect devourer can't use any of the host creature's spells with Body Thief but can use its own spells.

GCGNJYiHP5GMdDAQ.name: Exit Body
GCGNJYiHP5GMdDAQ.description:
Requirements The intellect devourer is controlling a body with Body Thief

Effect The intellect devourer leaves its host body, which dies instantly and is no longer a suitable host for any Body Thief ability. The intellect devourer appears at full size in an adjacent space.

pHckdhGspQesS1nl.name: Ravage
pHckdhGspQesS1nl.description:
The intellect devourer makes two talon Strikes against a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} creature, using the same attack modifier as its highest attack modifier.

These Strikes gain the death trait. If Ravage kills the target, the intellect devourer may use Body Thief against it as a free action.

wHsOiFn1P7QcaaJ2.name: Stolen Identity
wHsOiFn1P7QcaaJ2.description: While an intellect devourer uses Body Thief, it gains the ability to understand and speak all languages known by the host, as well as knowledge of the host body's abilities, identity, role in society, and personality. However, it does not gain the specific memories or knowledge of the host body.

The isqulug expels larvae from the hivemind in its head in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30]. Creatures in this area take @Damage[6d10[piercing]] damage as the swarm feeds on their flesh (@Check[type:reflex|dc:30|basic:true] save). Any creature that takes damage is exposed to isqulugia.

The isqulug becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for [[/br 1d4 #Stupified Duration]]{1d4 rounds}, during which it can't Expel Infestation.

The isqulug becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for [[/br 1d4 #Stupified Duration]]{1d4 rounds}, during which it can't Expel Infestation.

CwEVrNGC8oHJQrv0.name: Isqulugia
CwEVrNGC8oHJQrv0.description:
Isqulugia's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} conditions can't be removed until this affliction is removed

Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:30]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 hour)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (1 day)

Stage 3 fatigued and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 day)

Stage 4 paralyzed (1 day)

Stage 5 the creature dies, and its body violently transforms into a new isqulug. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish}, similar magic, or a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.kqhPt9344UkcGVYO]{Resurrect} ritual can return the victim to life. The new isqulug remains even if the victim is brought back to life.

Isqulugia.name: Isqulugia
Isqulugia.description:
Isqulugia's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} conditions can't be removed until this affliction is removed

Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:30]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 hour)

Stage 4 paralyzed (1 day)

Stage 5 the creature dies, and its body violently transforms into a new isqulug. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish}, similar magic, or a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.kqhPt9344UkcGVYO]{Resurrect} ritual can return the victim to life. The new isqulug remains even if the victim is brought back to life.

u3s5y4pE6L6HfEAq.name: Malleability
u3s5y4pE6L6HfEAq.description: The isqulug can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.kMcV8e5EZUxa6evt]{Squeeze} through tight spaces as if it were a Small creature. While Squeezing, it can move at its full Speed. The isqulug can even Squeeze through spaces that typically fit only a Tiny creature, but does so at the standard speed for Squeezing.

Malleability.name: Malleability
Malleability.description: The isqulug can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.kMcV8e5EZUxa6evt]{Squeeze} through tight spaces as if it were a Small creature. WtMCu7aRag8Z4ewh.name: Swamp Stride
WtMCu7aRag8Z4ewh.description: An isqulug ignores difficult terrain that's caused by typical features of swamps.

r7vHhfdRSulBJtov.name: Truth Vulnerability
r7vHhfdRSulBJtov.description: A glabrezu's lies sustain them, and the truth can destroy them. Whenever a revelation effect counteracts or reveals the glabrezu's illusions or the glabrezu fails a save against an effect that would prevent them from lying (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aewxsale5xWEPKLk]{Ring of Truth}), the demon takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage. They take this damage only once per effect, even if the effect reveals multiple illusions or prevents multiple lies.

Whenever a revelation effect counteracts or reveals the glabrezu's illusions or the glabrezu fails a save against an effect that would prevent them from lying (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aewxsale5xWEPKLk]{Ring of Truth}), the demon takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage. pl4CFdDU1X88olbS.name: Captive Rake
pl4CFdDU1X88olbS.description:
Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} in the glabrezu's pincer.

Effect The glabrezu pulls the grabbed creature up to 5 feet closer, then makes two claw Strikes against it. Those Strikes count as one attack when determining a treachery demon's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after both Strikes.

Captive Rake.name: Captive Rake
Captive Rake.description:

fRDoPX53siUmNijE.name: Steady Spellcasting
fRDoPX53siUmNijE.description: When a reaction would disrupt a glabrezu's spell, the demon can attempt a @Check[type:flat|dc:15] check, avoiding the disruption on a success.

8JslG7YLjptMOVC0.name: Sudden Betrayal
8JslG7YLjptMOVC0.description: A glabrezu can always use Deception when rolling initiative as long as they're aware of at least one opponent before initiative is rolled. When using Deception for initiative, the demon gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception.

bmbqZlQmEmeXm2DS.name: Treacherous Veil
bmbqZlQmEmeXm2DS.description: A glabrezu's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise} can allow it to appear as a creature of any size.

Treacherous Veil.name: Treacherous Veil
Treacherous Veil.description: A glabrezu's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise} can allow it to appear as a creature of any size.

nA5lgWKn3AryAuFs.name: Twisted Desires
nA5lgWKn3AryAuFs.description: Once per month, a glabrezu can grant a mortal humanoid the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.YfJTXyVGzLhM6V8U]{Miracle} spell granted by an Abyssal patron, though the glabrezu always perverts the miracle according to their own treacherous nature.

Twisted Desires.name: Twisted Desires
Twisted Desires.description: Once per month, a glabrezu can grant a mortal humanoid the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.YfJTXyVGzLhM6V8U]{Miracle} spell granted by an Abyssal patron, though the glabrezu always perverts the miracle according to their own treacherous nature.

MZkaydECJZaqZvYF.name: Nightmare Curse
MZkaydECJZaqZvYF.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Tiluatchek reaches out with his mind to infect the dreams of a single creature within 60 feet that he can see. The target suddenly experiences a brief but overwhelming memory of a harrowing nightmare it had at some point in the past and must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:32] save. The effects of this curse are permanent until the target is cured (although any frightened condition values imparted by the curse pass and end normally).

Critical Success The target is unaffected, and the nightmare vision passes as swiftly as it appears.

Success The vision causes the target to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}. The target also becomes cursed. Every time it attempts to sleep, it's plagued by nightmares of being stalked through an endless maze of caverns by something that wants to steal its body and eat its brain. The cursed creature now requires 10 hours of rest (rather than 8) in order to be able to make its daily preparations and can't take the Long-Term Rest activity at all.

Failure As success, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}, and each time the target sleeps, it suffers the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Uqj344bezBq3ESdq]{Nightmare} spell (@Check[type:will|dc:32] save).

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for as long as it remains frightened. If the cursed target ever rolls a critical failure against a nightmare spell while it remains cursed (including those caused by the curse itself), it dies in its sleep. If at the time of this death Tiluatchek is also dead (but for no more than 1 year), the intellect devourer is restored to life inside of the target creature's body as if he'd used Body Thief to take the body over.

If at the time of this death Tiluatchek is also dead (but for no more than 1 year), the intellect devourer is restored to life inside of the target creature's body as if he'd used Body Thief to take the body over.

AErcLCe3qBLb8HU0.name: No Breath
AErcLCe3qBLb8HU0.description: A rhu-chalik doesn't breathe and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as inhaled poisons).

lBss0k9IK6iCcQEo.name: Excruciating Enzyme
lBss0k9IK6iCcQEo.description: A rhu-chalik's tendrils secrete an enzyme that causes intense pain. A living creature hit by a tendril Strike must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:24] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain.

Excruciating Enzyme.name: Excruciating Enzyme
Excruciating Enzyme.description: A rhu-chalik's tendrils secrete an enzyme that causes intense pain. A living creature hit by a tendril Strike must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:24] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain.

JCVCYsMSue62ekID.name: Project Terror
JCVCYsMSue62ekID.description:
Requirements The rhu-chalik has successfully affected the target with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9BnhadUO8FMLmeZ3]{Mind Probe}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KHnhPHL4x1AQHfbC]{Mind Reading}, or Project Terror in the last minute

Effect The rhu-chalik creates nightmarish visions in the target's mind. The target must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to Project Terror for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}. Failing additional saves against this effect increases the frightened condition value by 2; if this would increase the target's frightened value beyond frightened 4, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4}.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} for 30 minutes instead of fleeing.

bScgZ7J4SQWXIPfp.name: Void Transmission
bScgZ7J4SQWXIPfp.description:
Requirements The rhu-chalik is adjacent to an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} creature

Effect The rhu-chalik spends 10 minutes copying the creature's entire consciousness and mentally sends this copied consciousness through the void of space to their waiting masters.

The target creature is deeply disoriented by this procedure, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 day afterward. If the creature is revived or moved away from the rhu-chalik during the process, Void Transmission fails and the target is unaffected.

fiU8SLNdNuWWwPbm.name: Pitfall
fiU8SLNdNuWWwPbm.description:
Trigger A creature walks onto the sheet of fungus

Effect The triggering creature falls 30 feet into area H7, taking @Damage[15[bludgeoning],6d6[piercing]]{15 falling damage and an additional 6d6 piercing} damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:24|basic:true] save) from the many stalagmites they land on. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} reaction to avoid falling (DC 24).

Pitfall.name: Pitfall
Pitfall.description:

IGWtUVFJMumVeibR.name: Swarming Bites
IGWtUVFJMumVeibR.description:
Trigger A creature moves into the room beyond the northern stairs

Effect A combination of biting flies and ravenous creepy-crawlies swarm the triggering creature. The creature takes @Damage[6d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:30|basic:true] save). If the creature took any damage, it's automatically exposed to ghast fever. The hazard then rolls initiative.

eTpo3PPNbe4sFYhD.name: Spore Explosion
eTpo3PPNbe4sFYhD.description:
Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if it's in direct sunlight or if the damage was fire damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within @Template[type:emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} are exposed to yellow mold spores.

tyAeSBnHmP9Nuh2k.name: Yellow Mold Spores
tyAeSBnHmP9Nuh2k.description:
Any drained condition from the spores persists after the poison's duration ends.

uf75VmCg2lXJXIS6.name: Spore Pop
uf75VmCg2lXJXIS6.description: If a myceloid is reduced to 0 HP by a critical hit, it pops, forcing it to immediately Emit Spores, even if it has already used the ability that day.

Spore Pop.name: Spore Pop
Spore Pop.description: If a myceloid is reduced to 0 HP by a critical hit, it pops, forcing it to immediately Emit Spores, even if it has already used the ability that day.

7O5f2VrYGu8KYH37.name: Emit Spores
7O5f2VrYGu8KYH37.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The myceloid expels spores in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] centered on a corner of its own space. This cloud lasts until the start of the myceloid's next turn. Each creature that is in the cloud or enters it is exposed to purple pox.

Emit Spores.name: Emit Spores

Frequency once per day

TjHnriTSE9y88Oaj.name: Purple Pox
TjHnriTSE9y88Oaj.description:
Myceloids are immune

Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20]

Onset 1 minute

Stage 1 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3}, and the creature is compelled to seek out the nearest myceloid colony-this compulsion is a mental emotion effect (1 day)

Stage 3 The creature dies. Over 24 hours, its corpse becomes bloated and bursts, releasing a new, fully grown myceloid.

Myceloids are immune

Stage 3 The creature dies. Over 24 hours, its corpse becomes bloated and bursts, releasing a new, fully grown myceloid.

Jyzm7OFRyidSiJk0.name: Spore Domination
Jyzm7OFRyidSiJk0.description:
The myceloid targets one creature affected by purple pox within 60 feet. That creature must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:22] save.

It is then temporarily immune to spore domination for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success Until the end of its next turn, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.v44P3WUcU1j0115l]{Helpful} to myceloids and can't take hostile actions against them.

Failure As success, but for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As success, but until the purple pox is cured.

1mqppqclycua1e1x.name: Power of the Haunt
1mqppqclycua1e1x.description: If a geist is within 100 feet of a haunt that isn't disabled or destroyed, they gain a +2 status bonus to AC and saving throws and deal an additional @Damage[1d6[void]] damage with bite Strikes. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[type:perception|dc:20|traits:action:seek] check to Seek or Recall Knowledge about the geist sees that the tendrils from the geist's cloak grow thicker and more agitated the closer it gets to the haunt.

o8cbkspx01eskehx.name: Terrifying Laugh
o8cbkspx01eskehx.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The geist unleashes soul-piercing laughter in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], lasting until the beginning of their next turn. Any creature in the area or entering it must succeed at a @Check[type:will|dc:26] save or be overcome with panicked laughter, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). A creature attempts only one save per Terrifying Laugh, and a creature that succeeds on its saving throw is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

A creature attempts only one save per Terrifying Laugh, and a creature that succeeds on its saving throw is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

58ox9o6ljire079b.name: Wrath of the Haunt
58ox9o6ljire079b.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The geist is within 100 feet of an active haunt

Effect The haunt feeds necromantic power into the geist, becoming inactive until the end of the geist's next turn and deactivating the power of the haunt aura until the haunt becomes active again. The edges of the geist's cloak transform into whip-like tendrils that appear to be made of fire and smoke and lash out at nearby creatures. Any living creature within 10 feet of the geist takes @Damage[4d10[fire],4d6[void]]{4d10 fire damage plus 4d6 void damage} (@Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true] save).

TpEz65tv1dwJ91cy.name: Alchemical Chambers
TpEz65tv1dwJ91cy.description:
An alchemical golem's body contains six alchemical chambers filled with different substances. When an alchemical golem ability calls upon a randomly determined alchemical effect, roll 1d6 and consult the following:

[[/br 1d6 #Alchemical Chambers]] Alchemical Effect
1

Acid Damage
2 Cold Damage
3 Electricity Damage
4 Fire Damage
5 Poison Damage
6 Sickness: @Check[type:fortitude|dc:26|name:Alchemical Sickness|traits:disease] save or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure)

N0g5xdIF3LC3JDxP.name: Alchemical Rupture
N0g5xdIF3LC3JDxP.description:
When an alchemical golem takes physical damage from a critical hit or is affected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.1xbFBQDRs0hT5xZ9]{Shatter} spell, one glass chamber within its body shatters, spewing alchemical liquid in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]{5-foot emanation}.

Roll on the alchemical chambers list to determine which one shatters-on a roll of 1-5, creatures in the area take 10d6 damage of the appropriate type (@Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true]). On a roll of 6, creatures must instead save against the sickness effect.

@Damage[10d6[acid]] damage @Check[type:reflex|dc:28|basic:true|traits:acid]

ahtVNJjKjna3LTc1.description:
When an alchemical golem hits a creature with a syringe Strike, roll 1d6 on the alchemical chambers list to determine the additional effect of the attack. The syringe deals an additional 2d6 damage of the appropriate type (or exposes the target to the sickness effect, as appropriate).

IUmBG7HtxvrIOHUR.name: Generate Bomb
IUmBG7HtxvrIOHUR.description:
The golem fills an empty vial from one of its alchemical chambers to create a bomb and then makes a bomb Strike.

Determine the type of bomb created by rolling 1d6 on the alchemical chambers list.

On a roll of 1-4, it creates the corresponding greater alchemical bomb: an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.jC8GmH0Un6vDxdMj]{Acid Flask} from chamber 1, a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.intVPcEzbE9o4NQd]{Frost Vial} from chamber 2, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.r2iTRbt1zpkAqHj2]{Bottled Lightning} from chamber 3, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.n5yTlTs63o53n3k6]{Alchemist's Fire} from chamber 4.

On a roll of 5, it creates a poisonous bomb that deals 3d10 poison damage and 3 poison splash damage with no other effects.

On a roll of 6, it creates a sickness bomb, which exposes the target and all creatures in the splash radius to the sickness effect; creatures hit by only the splash receive a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude saves.

[[/br 1d6 #Generate Bomb]] Alchemical Effect
1

Acid Flask: @Damage[1[acid],3d6[persistent,acid],(3[splash])[acid]] damage
2 Frost Vial: @Damage[3d6[cold],(3[splash])[cold]] damage, and -10-foot status penalty to Speeds until end of targets next turn @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.nJRoiSyd67eQ1dYj]{Effect: Frost Vial (Greater)}
3 Bottled Lightning: @Damage[3d6[electricity],(3[splash])[electricity]] damage, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until start of targets next turn
4 Alchemist's Fire: @Damage[3d8[fire],3[persistent,fire],(3[splash])[fire]] damage
5 Poisonous Bomb: @Damage[3d10[poison],(3[splash])[poison]] damage
6 Sickness: @Check[type:fortitude|dc:26|name:Alchemical Sickness|traits:disease] save or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure)

Determine the type of bomb created by rolling 1d6 on the alchemical chambers list.

On a roll of 1-4, it creates the corresponding greater alchemical bomb: an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.jC8GmH0Un6vDxdMj]{Acid Flask} from chamber 1, a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.intVPcEzbE9o4NQd]{Frost Vial} from chamber 2, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.r2iTRbt1zpkAqHj2]{Bottled Lightning} from chamber 3, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.n5yTlTs63o53n3k6]{Alchemist's Fire} from chamber 4.

On a roll of 5, it creates a poisonous bomb that deals 3d10 poison damage and 3 poison splash damage with no other effects.

On a roll of 6, it creates a sickness bomb, which exposes the target and all creatures in the splash radius to the sickness effect; creatures hit by only the splash receive a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude saves.

lgB1MU6GHJHRtB1m.name: Fungaltongue
lgB1MU6GHJHRtB1m.description: A fungus tyrant can speak with all forms of fungus, be they fungal creatures or otherwise. Most normal fungi don't recognize details about creatures or know anything about the world beyond their immediate vicinity. A fungus tyrant gains a +3 circumstance bonus on all Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation checks made against fungus creatures.

Fungaltongue.name: Fungaltongue
